subtopic: CAG and Attorney General of India
title: CAG and Attorney General of India
focus: Details the appointment, tenure, and duties of the CAG and the Attorney General.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 025]
About CAG
- Constitutional Body appointed under Article 148 of Constitution by President of India.
- CAG is not eligible for reappointment in central or state government.
- The extent of his duties is listed out in CAGs (Duties, Powers and Conditions of Service) Act, 1971.
- Tenure: 6 years or up to age of 65 years whichever is earlier.
- Removal: Removed by the President on the same grounds and in the same manner as a judge of the Supreme Court.
- Other Key Detail:
- Duty of the CAG arises only after the expenditure has been incurred.
- CAG reports on the execution of projects or programs by the ministers are discussed by the Public Accounts Committee.
- Information from CAG reports can be used by investigating agencies to press charges in court of law.
2.7.2. ATTORNEY GENERAL OF INDIA
Recently, senior advocate R. Venkataramani was re-appointed as Attorney General of India for two years.
About Attorney General of India (AGI)
- Highest law officer in country.
- Constitutional Body under Article 76 which provides for office of Attorney General for India.
- Appointment: By President.
- Eligibility: Must be a person who is qualified to be appointed a judge of Supreme Court.
- i.e. Must be a citizen of India and have been a judge of some high court for five years or an advocate of some high court for ten years or an eminent jurist.
- Term of office: Not fixed by Constitution.
- Removal: Constitution does not contain the procedure and grounds for removal. He holds office during pleasure of President.
- Rights of AGI:
- Right of audience in all courts in Indian territory.
- Right to speak and take part in proceedings of both Houses or their joint sitting and any committee of Parliament of which he may be named a member, but without a right to vote.
- Enjoys all privileges and immunities that are available to a Member of Parliament.
- Limitations of AGI:
- Not advise or hold a brief against Government of India (GoI).
- Not defend accused persons in criminal prosecutions without permission of GoI.
- Not accept appointment as a director in any company or corporation without permission of GoI.
- Duties and Functions:
- Give advice to GoI upon such legal matters, which are...

- The extent of his duties is listed out in CAGs (Duties, Powers and Conditions of Service) Act, 1971.
- Tenure: 6 years or up to age of 65 years whichever is earlier.
